x4codes.hlp: Universita degli Studi della Campania, Caserta, Italy

x4freetext: 3MV Pelletron
A highly ordered pyrolytic graphite target (1 mm thick,
10 mm diameter, 99.99% pure) was mounted on
water-cooled ladder. It was encased in a plastic
3D-printed sphere with 25 X 25 mm2 ports for beam entry
Measures energy loss (dE) with a Havar window
(23 X 23 mm2 large, 2.6+/-0.2 um thick) cathode,
aluminized Mylar foil (52.5 X 52.5 mm2, 1.6+/-0.2 um
thick, metallized with 50 um/cm2 Al) as anode placed
116 mm behind Havar window and a Frisch grid made of
gold-coated tungsten wires (20 um diameter) with CF4
gas.
Silicon strip detector measures residual energy,
with (58 X 58 mm2 active area and 300+/-15 um
thickness as a single pad.
The results show only statistical errors.
- The systematic uncertainties are due to:
Accumulated charge (<1%)
Solid angles (0.9-1.2%)
Stopping power (~8%)
